{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6900","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6900/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6900/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6900/events","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/panel/issues/6900","id":2340461079,"node_id":"I_kwDOCLF6Q86LgJoX","number":6900,"title":"Retrieving information about client screen dimensions","user":{"login":"nenb","id":55434794,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU1NDM0Nzk0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/55434794?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb","html_url":"https://github.com/nenb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-06-07T13:26:54Z","updated_at":"2024-06-07T13:26:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I would like to set the `page_size` for the `Tabulator` widget based on the dimensions of a user's screen eg on a laptop perhaps I would only display 10 rows per page, but on a large computer monitor I might display 15-20 rows per page. It would be useful to be able to access information about the client screen dimensions in this scenario.

#### Describe the solution you'd like
Is it possible to add another attribute to `BrowserInfo` that also retrieves the screen dimensions? The `device_pixel_ratio` is very useful, but I believe that I also need information about the client window width and height.

#### Describe alternatives you've considered
I couldn't find any alternative ways to do this inside `panel` (without writing or extending a model). If there is an alternative, please point me in the right direction and close this feature request.

#### Additional context
-

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6684","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6684/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6684/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/6684/events","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/panel/issues/6684","id":2229454430,"node_id":"I_kwDOCLF6Q86E4sZe","number":6684,"title":"`oauth_expiry` cookie provides incorrect information when using `refresh_token` ","user":{"login":"nenb","id":55434794,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU1NDM0Nzk0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/55434794?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb","html_url":"https://github.com/nenb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nenb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-04-06T23:05:37Z","updated_at":"2024-04-06T23:05:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"#### ALL software version info

```
Python 3.9.18
[GCC 12.2.0] on linux
>>> import panel
>>> panel.__version__
'1.3.8'
```

Probably not specific to `panel` version though.

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior

I am using the `generic` OAuth handler. I am also using the [refresh token](https://panel.holoviz.org/how_to/authentication/access_tokens.html#refreshing-access-tokens) functionality.

The issue I face is that after the `access_token` has been refreshed, the `oauth_expiry` cookie appears to provide incorrect information (it still provides information relevant to the original `access_token`).

It's quite possible that I am doing something incorrect, and this is not in fact a bug. I had a quick look at the code to try and understand what was going on. 

It appears that the `oauth_expiry` cookie is set in a [single location](https://github.com/holoviz/panel/blob/5f77d4f85df7c30de4e784a11b449f7f41c06e07/panel/auth.py#L427) inside the `_on_auth` function. It also appears that this function is [not entered](https://github.com/holoviz/panel/blob/5f77d4f85df7c30de4e784a11b449f7f41c06e07/panel/auth.py#L243-L248) when handling a `refresh_token`. This suggests to me that this might actually be a bug ie the cookie is not updated. If I have misunderstood, I will try and share a MRE that should hopefully resolve the issue one way or another.

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

Not included.

- [x] I may be interested in making a pull request to address this -> perhaps as simple as removing the early return?

`pn.__version__ = '1.3.8'`

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior

I want to have a `pn.indicators.Gauge` widget that responsively resizes. I would like to set a minimum/maximum width. The current defaults prevent me from doing this. For example, the default width (300) is passed to the ECharts Gauge object, and I end up with a widget that overflows the surrounding container (see screenshot).

 I'm not entirely sure if this is an issue with `panel` or the Apache ECharts library.

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

```
g = pn.indicators.Gauge(min_width=100, max_width=250, sizing_mode="scale_both")

g.get_root().width  # 300, from defaults, this is what is passed to ECharts
g.get_root().min_width  # 100, limit for container
g.get_root().max_width  # 250, limit for container
```

#### Other

I'm not sure why all the widgets seem to have default values set for their `width`/`height` and `sizing_mode`=`fixed`. It seems like it would be more useful to have `None` as their default. This could be ignorance on my part though, and perhaps it's needed for some other reason.

A separate issue that I noticed when trying to configure this widget is that if `width` is present, and `sizing_mode` is `None`, then `min_width` is automatically changed to `width`. This confused me for a while until I saw [this](https://github.com/holoviz/panel/blob/baaf9814320d2edc0659cfb10bb14cacb6ba67c5/panel/reactive.py#L187-L190) piece of code. What's the rationale for this - is it necessary?

- [x] I may be interested in making a pull request to address this

#### Screenshots

![Screenshot 2024-01-28 at 15 23 05](https://github.com/holoviz/panel/assets/55434794/6495d1bf-a2bb-475e-bf0f-272f730717cb)
